info.description: This is a backend API for Golang Boilerplate
info.title: Golang Boilerplate API

get.description: Check if the service is running

parameters.description: Filter by action (exact or prefix with trailing dot)
parameters.name: action

get.summary: Get an OAuth client by ID (platform admin)
Get OAuth client usage metrics (platform admin)
Get a tenant by ID (platform admin)
List identity providers for a tenant

patch.summary: Configure an upstream identity provider for a tenant (platform admin)

get.summary: List members of a tenant (platform admin)

post.summary: Add an existing user to a tenant

delete.summary: Remove a user from a tenant

post.summary: Reset MFA (TOTP + recovery codes) for a user (platform admin)
Reset all passkeys for a user (platform admin)

get.summary: List enabled federation sign-in methods for a tenant

get.description: Check if the database connection is healthy

patch.summary: Configure an upstream identity provider for a tenant

post.description: Sets the same iam_session cookie as OIDC so /authorize recognizes the browser without a second login. If the user has MFA (TOTP) enabled, `data` is MFALoginChallengeResponse (mfa_ticket) instead of LoginResponse; complete login with POST /mfa/challenge/verify. When the user belongs to multiple tenants and no tenant context is provided, `data` is TenantSelectionResponse.

200.description: When MFA is off: LoginResponse. When MFA is on: same envelope but `data` is MFALoginChallengeResponse (see top-level description).

post.description: Reads the browser iam_session cookie set by federation callback and returns JWT tokens for the SPA dashboard. Used by GET /login/federation/complete.
post.summary: Exchange iam_session cookie for API tokens (after federated sign-in)

200.description: When tenant is resolved. When multiple tenants apply, `data` is TenantSelectionResponse.

post.summary: Log out everywhere for the current user (SSO session + all refresh tokens)

get.summary: List tenants the current user can access

post.description: Consumes the `mfa_ticket` from password or passkey login when MFA is required. `code` is a 6-digit TOTP, or a recovery code (non–6-digit path). On success returns LoginResponse and sets `iam_session` cookie.
post.summary: Complete MFA after password or passkey login

post.description: Replaces any existing recovery codes. Requires TOTP to be enabled. Plain codes are returned once; only hashes are stored.
Generates a TOTP secret, persists it (encrypted) as pending, and returns the raw secret plus otpauth URI for QR scanning. Call POST /mfa/totp/verify with a valid code to enable MFA.
Validates a 6-digit code against the pending secret and enables TOTP for the user.

parameters.description: URL to return to after login (must be /authorize on this app)
parameters.name: return_to

302.description: Redirect to return_to with session cookie set

post.summary: Exchange refresh token for a new access token (and rotated refresh token)
Complete login by selecting a tenant
Switch active tenant and re-issue tokens

get.summary: Userinfo endpoint (Bearer access token from token endpoint, RS256)

get.description: Returns passkeys registered for the current user (device label and created date only).

post.description: Verifies the assertion. On success without MFA: returns `data` as LoginResponse and sets `iam_session` cookie. When MFA is enabled for the user, returns MFALoginChallengeResponse in `data` instead (then call POST /mfa/challenge/verify).

200.description: When MFA is off: LoginResponse + iam_session. When MFA is on: `data` is MFALoginChallengeResponse (see description).

post.description: Returns WebAuthn PublicKeyCredentialRequestOptions as `data.options` and a `session_token` for login/finish. Public endpoint (rate limited).
Verifies the attestation and stores the passkey for the current user. Body includes `session_token` from register/start and the PublicKeyCredential JSON from the browser.
Returns WebAuthn PublicKeyCredentialCreationOptions as `data.options` and a `session_token` to send with register/finish. Requires a logged-in user (Bearer access token).

remember_me.description: RememberMe extends the iam_session cookie lifetime (SSO_SESSION_REMEMBER_TTL). API login and POST /oidc/login both honor this.

return_to.description: ReturnTo when set (OIDC browser flow): creates a session cookie and redirects here with 302 — no access_token in the response body.

credential.description: Credential is the browser PublicKeyCredential JSON from navigator.credentials.get().
Credential is the browser PublicKeyCredential JSON from navigator.credentials.create().

device_name.description: DeviceName optional label stored with the passkey (e.g. "Work MacBook").

BearerAuth.description: Bearer Token Authentication. Use "Bearer {token}" as the value.
BearerAuth.name: Authorization
